behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is at the `git` CLI -> parser boundary (real rename status formats and thresholds), not just one pure function. REAL-INFRA: requires real subprocess + real repo/index state. MOCK-HIDE: a stubbed `git diff --name-status` response can make parsing look correct while real `git` emits variants (`Rxx`, path separators, tabs) that break categorization. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Integration): an integration test with mocked git output passes for `R100 old new`, but fails in production when real git emits a different rename score/format and the tool double-counts. REJECTION-GATE: higher levels rejected because behavior text has no AI/ML dependency and no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit `a.txt`, rename to `b.txt`, run CLI against both staged and committed rename scenarios via real `git` subprocess, assert report has rename count incremented and add/delete counts unchanged for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: crash risk appears when real git emits binary-diff output/encodings across process boundaries. REAL-INFRA: real filesystem bytes + real `git diff` subprocess behavior are required. MOCK-HIDE: text-only fixture/mocked output can never reproduce decode/stream edge cases from actual binary blobs. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Integration): parser unit/integration with canned text passes, but real run crashes on non-UTF8/binary diff output. REJECTION-GATE: no behavior text requires model judgment or multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, add/modify binary files (e.g., random bytes image/blob), run CLI with real git state, assert process exits successfully and report includes binary file changes in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs from real git error semantics on unborn `HEAD` (exit code/stderr), a subprocess/OS interaction. REAL-INFRA: requires actual repo state with zero commits and real git invocation. MOCK-HIDE: mocked command success paths can hide incorrect handling of real non-zero exit codes. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Integration): integration test stubs `git log` as empty and passes; real run returns fatal/unborn-HEAD error and tool exits non-zero. REJECTION-GATE: no AI/ML output dependence; no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize an empty git repo with no commits, execute CLI, assert zero exit (or documented success behavior) and structured empty report on stdout instead of traceback/error output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: correctness depends on end-to-end reconciliation of real git history (adds/deletes/renames/modifies) through subprocess output and parsing. REAL-INFRA: requires real commit graph, index, and file operations; local fakes can miss git’s actual classification behavior. MOCK-HIDE: canned fixtures can be internally consistent but diverge from real git history semantics. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Integration): mocked repo adapter test passes against handcrafted events, while real repository with mixed rename+modify history yields wrong counts. REJECTION-GATE: behavior text is deterministic repository accounting, not model-quality evaluation and not multi-agent orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known operations across several commits (add/delete/rename/modify), run CLI, and compare each reported category count to expected counts derived from the constructed history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this behavior is specifically about git index vs working tree semantics across real command flags/states. REAL-INFRA: needs real filesystem + real git index/worktree interactions via subprocess. MOCK-HIDE: mocked status/diff responses can force equivalence and hide real divergence between staged/unstaged handling. ADJACENT-COUNTEREXAMPLE (Integration): test with fake diff provider returns same payload for both modes and passes; real repo run differs because CLI queries wrong git target (`--cached` vs working tree). REJECTION-GATE: no LLM dependency and no multi-step agentic pipeline in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent content changes once as staged-only and once as unstaged-only, run CLI in both modes, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout output.
